text.content:
Running This Chapter

Chapter 1 is designed as a sandbox. The characters can explore Ten-Towns in any order. Follow these steps:

  1. Before starting, read the rest of this section and the Ten-Towns Overview below.
  2. Determine the starting town (roll on the Starting Town table or choose) and read that town's description.
  3. Once players have their characters and secrets, kick off the adventure with the Cold Open.
  4. Give players a starting quest: either Cold-Hearted Killer, Nature Spirits, or the quest tied to their starting town.
  5. Whenever characters arrive at a new town for the first time, give them the quest tied to that settlement.

Character Advancement

  • Advance to 2nd level after completing their first quest.
  • Advance to 3rd level after completing three quests.
  • Advance to 4th level after completing five quests.

Once at 4th level, characters no longer gain levels from completing Ten-Towns quests, but completing more improves their reputation.

Reputation in Ten-Towns

  • At 3rd level, the party earns a healthy measure of respect from Ten-Towners who know of their exploits.
  • At 4th level, they receive a hero's welcome in towns they've helped but a cold shoulder in towns they've ignored. They also learn of new adventure opportunities outside Ten-Towns (Chapter 2).

Ten-Towns Overview

Ten-Towns began four centuries ago as a modest trade post atop the hill where Bryn Shander now stands. One by one, settlements sprung up on the shores of Maer Dualdon, Lac Dinneshere, and Redwaters. The three lakes and their ten settlements form the heart of civilization in Icewind Dale.

The Three Lakes

  • Maer Dualdon — The deepest lake, with the largest population of knucklehead trout. Four towns sit on its shores: Bremen, Lonelywood, Targos, and Termalaine.
  • Redwaters — The shallowest lake, almost completely freezing in winter. Three towns: Good Mead, Dougan's Hole, and Bryn Shander (inland, on a hill).
  • Lac Dinneshere — Catches the worst winds from the Reghed Glacier. Three towns: Caer-Konig, Caer-Dineval, and Easthaven.

Snowflake Ratings

Each town is rated on three qualities using one to three snowflakes (❄):

  • Friendliness — How welcoming residents are to strangers.
  • Services — Availability of goods, lodging, and spellcasting.
  • Comfort — Quality of food, drink, and accommodation.

Getting Around Ten-Towns

Most Ten-Towners travel on foot. Those transporting goods use wooden sleds pulled by dogs or domesticated axe beaks.

ItemCostWeight
Clothing, cold weather10 gp5 lb.
Crampons (2)2 gp1/4 lb.
Snowshoes2 gp4 lb.
Sled (empty)20 gp300 lb.
Sled dog50 gp—
Axe beak (domesticated)50 gp—

Sacrifices to Auril

The desperate people of Ten-Towns make sacrifices to Auril on nights of the new moon, hoping to appease the Frostmaiden. The nature of sacrifices varies by town:

  • Humanoid — Bryn Shander, Easthaven, and Targos hold lotteries. The unlucky soul is stripped bare and sent into the tundra to die.
  • Food — Smaller towns string up a day's catch of knucklehead trout a mile outside town.
  • Warmth — Some towns forsake all fires from dusk to dawn, sharing body heat for warmth.

Cold Open

Read the following to players when beginning the adventure:

Icewind Dale has become trapped in a perpetual winter. Ferocious blizzards make the mountain pass through the Spine of the World exceedingly treacherous, and this land has not felt the warmth of the sun in over two years. In fact, the sun no longer appears above the mountains, not even in what should be the height of summer. In this frozen tundra, darkness and bitter cold reign as king and queen. Most dale residents blame Auril the Frostmaiden, the god of winter's wrath.

Dalefolk live in a scattering of settlements known as Ten-Towns. The drop-off in caravans coming from the south and travel between settlements in this never-ending winter has left everyone feeling isolated. Although each town has resolved to appease the Frostmaiden with sacrifices of one kind or another, no respite from winter's fury seems forthcoming. For adventurers such as yourselves, Ten-Towns is a place to test one's mettle and, in the spirit of heroes who have come before, leave one's mark on this frigid, blighted land.

Starting Quests

Cold-Hearted Killer

The characters receive this quest from Hlin Trollbane, a retired bounty hunter (neutral good shield dwarf, unarmored veteran) who has been stalking a serial killer. She hires the characters to apprehend her suspect, Sephek Kaltro, and dispose of him if guilty.

What Hlin knows:

  • Sephek works for a small traveling merchant company called Torg's, owned by a shady dwarf named Torrga Icevein.
  • His victims come only from Bryn Shander, Easthaven, and Targos — the three towns that sacrifice people to the Frostmaiden.
  • Sephek doesn't wear cold weather clothing and seems immune to the cold.

Reward: 100 gp on delivery of proof of Sephek's death.

Sephek Kaltro is a possessed vessel — a mariner whose body was taken over by a frost druid spirit in service to Auril. He wields ice weapons, has cold damage immunity, regenerates in cold temperatures, and can cast misty step three times per day.

Nature Spirits

The characters receive this quest from Dannika Graysteel (lawful neutral half-elf acolyte), a scholar who believes chwingas — tiny elemental spirits — might hold the key to improving Icewind Dale's climate.

Task: Locate and capture a chwinga (or convince one to help). Dannika provides a lantern of tracking (elementals) to help find them.

Reward: 25 gp, plus the lantern to keep. Bonus 25 gp if the chwinga was not harmed.

Finding Chwingas: No chwingas are in the starting town, but each subsequent town visited has a cumulative 25% chance of having chwingas present. When found, 1d3 chwingas are playing in a clearing — use the "Playing House" encounter.

Ten-Towns at a Glance

TownLakePopulationSpeakerSacrificeQuest

EasthavenLac Dinneshere~750Danneth Waylen (human)HumanoidToil and Trouble
Good MeadRedwaters~100Vacant (Mead Must Flow quest)WarmthThe Mead Must Flow

TargosMaer Dualdon~1,000Naerth Maxildanarr (human)HumanoidMountain Climb

See individual town journals for full details on each settlement, NPCs, locations, and quests.
